cards.goal: Activate rest-and-digest mode with cold water, tongue scraping, warm water, and evening humming.

cards.title: How this program works
paragraphs: This free 6-day journey addresses six root patterns that affect sleep, energy, gut health, stress, and addictive habits.
Each day adds one simple practice. Nothing to buy. No supplements. No equipment. Keep the practices gentle and consistent.
This program is for general wellness. If you have a diagnosed medical condition or concerning symptoms, consult your doctor before starting.
cards.highlight: Do the days in order. Each practice builds on the previous one.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Your nervous system has two modes: fight-or-flight and rest-and-digest. Stress, screens, caffeine, and modern pace keep many people in fight-or-flight for too long.
Today you deliberately signal rest-and-digest with three morning tools and one evening close. Together they take only a few minutes.
cards.highlight: The first signal is simple: cold water, a clean mouth, warm water, and a calm evening vibration.

cards.title: Cold water face splash
instructions: Go to your sink after waking.
Splash cold water on your face 5 to 7 times.
Do this before tea, food, your phone, or any other morning habit.
cards.whyThisWorks: Cold water on the face triggers the dive reflex and wakes the vagus nerve, the direct connection between your brain, gut, heart rate, stress response, and sleep quality.
cards.proTip: Use this again if stress or cravings spike later in the day.

cards.title: Tongue scraping
instructions: Before brushing, scrape your tongue from back to front 5 to 7 times.
Use a tongue scraper or the back edge of a metal spoon.
Rinse with warm water, then brush normally.
cards.whyThisWorks: The mouth and gut share the same system. Removing overnight coating reduces the bacteria you swallow with your first drink.

cards.title: 500 ml warm water on waking
instructions: After tongue scraping, drink 500 ml warm water slowly over 2 to 3 minutes.
Keep it warm, not hot and not cold.
From today, avoid cold water and cold drinks during the program.
cards.whyThisWorks: Warm water triggers the gut's natural morning clearing reflex. Cold water can tighten gut muscle and disrupt digestion.

cards.title: What this means for your focus
paragraphs: Better Sleep: Vagus nerve activation supports sleep onset by moving the body toward rest-and-digest mode.
More Energy: Cold water can increase alertness quickly without relying on caffeine.
Gut Health: The vagus nerve helps gut movement, digestion, and morning bowel rhythm.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: Cold water face splash can interrupt craving intensity fast enough to create a pause.
Stress Relief: Cold water gives the body a quick cortisol reset signal.
General Wellness: Warm water on waking supports rehydration and the body's morning clearing process.

cards.title: Water schedule for today
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm water before anything else.
30 minutes before breakfast: 200 ml warm water.
Mid-morning: 200 ml warm water or warm herbal tea.
30 minutes before lunch: 200 ml warm water.
Afternoon: 300 ml warm water or jeera water.
30 minutes before dinner: 200 ml warm water.
Total target: 2 litres minimum, all warm or room temperature.
cards.proTip: Eat your normal meals today. The only change is replacing cold drinks with warm or room-temperature water.

cards.title: Evening humming
instructions: Before bed, hum any low steady note for 2 minutes.
Feel the vibration in your throat and chest.
Keep it quiet and relaxed.
cards.whyThisWorks: Humming creates vibration around the throat and stimulates the vagus nerve again before sleep.

cards.prompt: Where did I notice my nervous system today, before or after the cold water?
cards.helperText: One honest sentence is enough, or simply think about it before sleep.

cards.title: Day 1 completion checklist
checklistItems: Cold water face splash on waking
Hum any note for 2 minutes before bed
cards.checklistQuote: The body changes faster than the mind believes it can.

cards.secondaryMessage: You gave your body its first clear reset signal.

cards.goal: Upgrade your morning protocol and fix water timing through the day.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Many people are mildly dehydrated without feeling thirsty. That can show up as fatigue, poor gut function, disrupted sleep, amplified stress, and stronger cravings.
Today adds oil pulling and structured water timing. The mouth is the gut's gateway, so clearing the mouth changes what reaches the gut.
cards.highlight: Clear the mouth first, then hydrate with timing and temperature.

cards.title: Morning protocol: four steps
instructions: Oil pulling: swish 1 tablespoon sesame or coconut oil for 5 minutes. Do not swallow.
Spit into a bin, not the sink.
Tongue scraping: 5 to 7 strokes.
Cold water face splash: 5 to 7 splashes.
500 ml warm water: drink slowly before food.
cards.whyThisWorks: Oil pulling removes bacteria first. Scraping clears residue. Cold water activates the vagus nerve. Warm water triggers the bowel reflex and rehydrates the body.

cards.title: Structured water timing
instructions: Before meals: drink 200 ml warm water 30 minutes before eating.
During meals: take only small sips, not large glasses.
After meals: wait 20 minutes, then drink 200 ml warm water.
All day: no cold drinks.
cards.whyThisWorks: Water at the wrong time or temperature can worsen bloating and low energy. Correct timing supports digestive enzymes and bowel movement.

cards.title: What this means for your focus
paragraphs: Better Sleep: Hydration supports melatonin production and deeper sleep.
More Energy: Mild dehydration is a common cause of the afternoon energy crash.
Gut Health: The colon needs water to keep stool soft and moving.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: The brain can confuse dehydration signals with craving signals.
Stress Relief: Dehydration can elevate cortisol and make the body feel stressed.
General Wellness: Oil pulling reduces harmful oral bacteria before they travel downstream.

cards.title: Water schedule for today
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm water after morning protocol.
30 minutes before breakfast: 200 ml warm water.
During meals: small sips only.
30 minutes before lunch: 200 ml warm water.
After lunch: 200 ml warm water after waiting 20 minutes.
Mid-afternoon: 300 ml jeera water.
30 minutes before dinner: 200 ml warm water.
After dinner: 200 ml warm water.
Total target: 2.5 litres, all warm or room temperature.
cards.proTip: Jeera water: boil 1 teaspoon cumin seeds in 300 ml water for 5 minutes, strain, and drink warm.

cards.prompt: Did water before meals feel different from water during meals? How did my afternoon energy feel?

cards.title: Day 2 completion checklist
checklistItems: Evening humming and warm salt-water gargle
cards.checklistQuote: Most fatigue is dehydration wearing a disguise.

cards.secondaryMessage: You strengthened hydration, mouth health, and digestive timing.

cards.goal: Use morning sunlight and post-meal walking to anchor your body clock.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Morning sunlight and movement are two of the strongest free health tools available. Together they set the 24-hour rhythm that governs energy, sleep, gut bacteria activity, cortisol, and cravings.
Today you build the morning anchor your body can repeat.
cards.highlight: Light tells the body what time it is. Movement tells the body how to use that signal.

cards.title: Morning sunlight walk
instructions: Within 30 minutes of waking, go outside.
Walk in direct outdoor light for 10 minutes. Do not count light through glass.
Skip sunglasses for the first 10 minutes if safe.
Barefoot on grass or soil is optional if available.
If it is raining, still step outside for light under cover.
cards.whyThisWorks: Morning sunlight sets the circadian clock, supports a healthy cortisol peak, initiates vitamin D production, and helps serotonin convert to melatonin later at night.

cards.title: Post-meal walks
instructions: After lunch, walk at a comfortable pace for 10 minutes.
After dinner, repeat the same 10-minute walk.
Keep the pace gentle and conversational.
Breathe through your nose during the walk.
cards.whyThisWorks: Walking stimulates the gut's muscular movement, supports blood sugar after eating, and improves blood flow to digestive organs.

cards.title: What this means for your focus
paragraphs: Better Sleep: Morning light sets tonight's melatonin timing.
More Energy: Sunlight gives genuine morning alertness and post-meal walking prevents the afternoon crash.
Gut Health: Walking after meals supports the gut's natural cleaning wave.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: A 10-minute walk can reduce craving intensity by giving stress hormones a physical outlet.
Stress Relief: Movement metabolizes cortisol and adrenaline.
General Wellness: Morning sunlight, grounding, and easy movement are a high-value daily foundation.

cards.title: Water schedule for today
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm water after morning protocol.
30 minutes before breakfast: 200 ml warm water.
Carry warm water during the morning walk if possible.
30 minutes before lunch: 200 ml warm water.
Mid-afternoon: 300 ml jeera or ajwain water.
30 minutes before dinner: 200 ml warm water.
Total target: 2.5 litres, all warm or room temperature.
cards.proTip: Do not skip breakfast today. Eat within 45 minutes of your morning walk if possible.

cards.prompt: How did my body feel after the morning walk and sunlight? Did the post-meal walk change how I felt after eating?

cards.title: Day 3 completion checklist
checklistItems: Breakfast within 45 minutes of the walk
Post-meal walk after lunch for 10 minutes
Post-meal walk after dinner for 10 minutes
cards.checklistQuote: The body was designed to move. Every walk is a conversation with it.

cards.secondaryMessage: You gave your day a stronger morning and movement anchor.

cards.goal: Support digestion with chewing, warmth, and a short gut yoga sequence.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: The gut produces much of the body's serotonin, supports immunity, and sends signals back to the brain.
Today introduces three gut practices: chewing, a warm belly compress, and an 8-minute yoga sequence. They target enzyme activation, blood flow, and gut movement.
cards.highlight: Support the gut mechanically before adding more complicated changes.

cards.title: The chewing practice
instructions: Choose your biggest meal today.
Put your phone away and sit down properly.
For the first 10 bites, chew each bite 20 times before swallowing.
After 10 bites, eat the rest of the meal normally.
cards.whyThisWorks: Digestion starts in the mouth. More chewing gives saliva enzymes time to begin breakdown before food reaches the stomach.

cards.title: Warm belly compress
instructions: After your largest meal, wet a small towel with warm water and wring it out.
Sit or lie comfortably.
Place the warm cloth from the belly button to just below the ribs.
Leave it for 5 minutes and breathe slowly.
Keep the cloth warm and comfortable, not hot.
cards.whyThisWorks: Warmth increases blood flow to the digestive organs and can reduce the heavy feeling after food.

cards.title: 8-minute gut yoga sequence
instructions: Knees to chest for 2 minutes: lie on your back, pull both knees to your chest, and rock gently side to side.
Seated spinal twist for 1 minute each side: sit cross-legged, twist gently, and hold for 30 seconds each.
Child's pose for 1 minute: kneel, sit on your heels, fold forward, and extend your arms.
Cat and cow for 1 minute: on hands and knees, arch your back up, then drop your belly down. Alternate 10 times.
Lying twist for 30 seconds each side: lie on your back, drop both knees to one side, look the opposite way, then repeat.
cards.whyThisWorks: These poses compress and release sections of the digestive tract, helping trapped gas, gut movement, and belly relaxation.

cards.title: What this means for your focus
paragraphs: Better Sleep: Gut serotonin is a precursor to melatonin, so digestion supports sleep chemistry.
More Energy: Better digestion improves how much energy you can extract from food.
Gut Health: Chewing, warmth, and movement address three separate gut dysfunction patterns.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: Gut inflammation can disturb dopamine regulation and increase reward-seeking.
Stress Relief: The gut-brain axis means gut tension can amplify stress reactivity.
General Wellness: Gut function supports immunity, mood, energy, sleep, and skin health.

cards.title: Water and nutrition note
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm water after morning protocol.
Mid-afternoon: 300 ml ajwain water.
No large glasses during meals. Use small sips only.
After dinner: 200 ml warm water after waiting 20 minutes.
Add one fermented food to at least one meal: homemade curd, chaas, idli, or dosa from fermented batter.
Add a small piece of fresh grated ginger to your morning water if it suits you.
cards.proTip: Skip anything that does not suit your body. The goal is support, not force.

cards.prompt: How did the chewing practice change the meal? Did the warm compress reduce post-meal heaviness?

cards.title: Day 4 completion checklist
checklistItems: Chewing practice at the biggest meal
Warm compress after the biggest meal
Fermented food added to at least one meal
Post-meal walks after lunch and dinner
cards.checklistQuote: The gut is not just where food goes. It is where health begins.

cards.secondaryMessage: You supported digestion with attention, warmth, and movement.

cards.goal: Use the physiological sigh, pattern awareness, and body mapping to identify stored stress.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: Unprocessed stress can store in the body as tension, hormonal disruption, and nervous-system dysregulation.
Today you work with two layers: a fast body reset and the awareness of what triggers your primary issue.
cards.highlight: Do not solve everything today. Name the pattern and give the body a calmer signal.

cards.title: Physiological sigh
cards.instructions: Take a normal breath in through the nose, add a second small sip of air, then release a long slow exhale through the mouth. Repeat 3 times.

cards.title: Pattern awareness
instructions: Sit quietly with no phone or distraction.
Ask: When does my primary issue get worst?
Ask: What emotion is usually present just before it peaks?
Ask: What situation or event triggers it most reliably?
Ask: What am I looking for when the urge or symptom appears?
Write one sentence for each, or hold the answers in your mind.
cards.whyThisWorks: You cannot change a pattern you cannot see. Noticing the trigger-response loop begins to interrupt automatic reactions.

cards.title: Body tension mapping
steps: Lie on your back and close your eyes.
Scan slowly from throat to lower belly.
Notice any tight, contracted, or uncomfortable area.
Place one hand there and take 5 slow breaths.
Do not force a release. Just notice and breathe.
cards.completionMessage: You mapped the body without trying to fight it.

cards.title: What this means for your focus
paragraphs: Better Sleep: Racing thoughts at night often come from a pattern that has not been named.
More Energy: Chronic stress diverts energy into a constant stress response.
Gut Health: Emotional stress changes gut motility, enzymes, and gut bacteria balance.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: Most relapses come from emotional triggers that peak before the user sees them.
Stress Relief: The physiological sigh is a fast reset, and pattern awareness helps you intervene earlier.
General Wellness: Knowing when, where, and why the body feels worst helps you respond before symptoms peak.

cards.title: Water and nutrition note
instructions: On waking: 500 ml warm ginger water.
30 minutes before each meal: 200 ml warm water.
Mid-afternoon: 300 ml jeera water.
Before bed: 150 ml warm turmeric milk if it suits you.
For sleep: avoid chai or coffee after 2 PM.
For energy: include protein at breakfast.
For gut: add turmeric with black pepper to one meal.
For quit support: replace one post-meal craving with saunf and a 2-minute walk.

cards.prompt: What is my most reliable trigger for my primary health issue? What emotion is usually present just before it appears?

cards.title: Day 5 completion checklist
checklistItems: Physiological sigh used at least once
cards.checklistQuote: What you can name, you can change. What you cannot see controls you.

cards.secondaryMessage: You gave your mind and body a clearer map.

cards.goal: Combine the six days into one complete daily rhythm and choose the next full program.

cards.title: What today is about
paragraphs: The body works on a 24-hour clock. Sleep, digestion, hormone production, immunity, and gut bacteria all run on rhythm.
Today you run everything from the week as one complete routine, then connect it to the full Recovery Compass program for your primary focus.
cards.highlight: The win is not doing more. The win is creating a rhythm you can repeat.

cards.title: Complete morning routine
instructions: Morning belly check for 2 minutes.
Oil pulling for 5 minutes.
Tongue scraping for 30 seconds.
Cold water face splash for 10 seconds.
700 ml warm ginger water before food.
Morning sunlight walk for 10 minutes.
cards.whyThisWorks: This sequence combines nervous-system activation, oral-gut hygiene, hydration, and circadian anchoring.

cards.title: Complete evening routine
instructions: Post-dinner walk for 10 minutes.
Warm compress on the belly for 5 minutes.
Gut yoga sequence for 8 minutes.
Physiological sigh for 3 cycles.
Body tension mapping for 3 minutes.
Warm turmeric milk or chamomile tea if it suits you.
Humming for 2 minutes and gargling for 30 seconds.
cards.whyThisWorks: The evening routine closes the day with digestion, movement, breathing, and vagus-nerve calming signals.

cards.title: Start the 12-hour eating window
instructions: Note what time you eat breakfast today.
Your last food should be 12 hours after your first meal.
Example: if breakfast is at 8 AM, last food is at 8 PM.
After the window closes, use warm water, herbal tea, or jeera water only.
cards.whyThisWorks: The gut bacteria clock responds to consistent eating windows within days. A clear overnight rest window supports digestion and sleep.

cards.title: What you built in 6 days
paragraphs: Day 1: Vagus nerve activation with cold water, tongue scraping, warm water, and humming.
Day 2: Hydration reset with oil pulling, water timing, jeera water, and no cold drinks.
Day 3: Movement anchor with morning sunlight and post-meal walks.
Day 4: Gut reset with chewing, warm compress, gut yoga, and fermented foods.
Day 5: Mind reset with physiological sigh, pattern awareness, and body mapping.
Day 6: Daily rhythm with morning routine, evening routine, and a 12-hour eating window.

cards.title: Your next full program
paragraphs: Better Sleep: continue with Deep Sleep Reset.
More Energy: continue with Energy Restore.
Gut Health: continue with Gut Reset.
Quit Smoking or Alcohol: continue with Smoking & Alcohol Quit.
Stress Relief or General Wellness: continue with the full Recovery Compass long-form reset when available.
cards.highlight: These 6 days address root causes. The full programs add deeper tools and daily structure.

cards.title: Six permanent habits
instructions: Morning sunlight walk to set the body clock.
Tongue scraping and warm water for oral-gut protection.
Post-meal walk for gut movement, blood sugar, and energy.
Chewing practice at one meal.
Physiological sigh whenever stress peaks.
12-hour eating window to reset the gut bacteria clock.
cards.proTip: Keep the whole program available and revisit this quick reference whenever you need structure.

cards.prompt: Looking back at 6 days, what is the one practice that made the most noticeable difference? What will I keep permanently?

cards.title: Day 6 completion checklist
checklistItems: Chewing practice at the biggest meal
Warm compress after the biggest meal
Next full Recovery Compass program identified
cards.checklistQuote: Six days of small signals. The body has been listening the whole time.

cards.message: Your Free Detox Program is complete.
cards.secondaryMessage: Return to Home when you are ready and choose the full journey that matches your focus.
